title: The Sand-Shelf Library

features.name: endless-shelf
features.desc-on-touch: Shelves rise and recede into the heat shimmer, every spine a grain of sand, every page a beach. The archive is reading itself into archive.

features.name: book-made-beach
features.desc-on-touch: A single volume lies open, and you realize it is the shore itself—pages of compressed sand, ink of crushed shells, the binding a tidal rhythm.

features.name: voice-of-beach
features.desc-on-touch: The archive's voice emerges from the sand, every grain a phoneme. You are reading the shore by becoming its sound.

features.name: page-grain
features.desc-on-touch: Individual sand grains shimmer with text. Every grain holds a word. Every word holds a beach that remembers falling.

features.name: dusk-reading
features.desc-on-touch: The light here is eternal dusk—that moment when sand becomes ink, when shadow and gold are the same colour.

inscription: the archive reads itself into the strand; every book a beach, every grain a page that remembers falling

file.name: Shelf_Coordinates_Archive_Beach_Integration_Protocols.pdf
file.note: How to shelve an ocean. Sand as paper, tide as binding. The library that has learned to be a shore.

exits.label: back into the room where sand still holds separate pages
toward the horizon where shelf and water become one question
into the deeper shelf, where pages have begun to grow gills
